diagrams.description: This timeline traces the development of International Law from its early modern roots to contemporary global governance. Key milestones include: Hugo Grotius’s foundational work De Jure Belli ac Pacis (1625) establishing natural law frameworks; the Peace of Westphalia (1648) recognizing sovereign equality of states; the Congress of Vienna (1815) codifying diplomatic law; the First Geneva Convention (1864) for humanitarian protection; the Hague Peace Conferences (1899, 1907); the creation of the League of Nations (1919) and the United Nations Charter (1945); the establishment of the International Court of Justice (1946); the four Geneva Conventions (1949); the Vienna Convention on the Law of Treaties (1969); the Rome Statute creating the International Criminal Court (1998); and the Paris Agreement on climate change (2015). The timeline illustrates the progressive codification and expansion of international law from bilateral diplomacy to multilateral institutions and universal jurisdiction.
